title: Announcing our $800M Series C to accelerate the shift to open-source AI
gist: Together AI raised an $800M Series C plus commitments for 500+ MW of independently-capitalized compute, betting that as AI moves into production the compounding inference costs of closed frontier models push companies toward open-weights models that deliver 6x-20x lower cost at equal-or-better performance.

insights.claim: Together AI raised an $800M Series C from investors including Aramco Ventures, NVIDIA, Vista Equity, General Catalyst, Schneider Electric, Pegatron, and Salesforce Ventures, plus separate commitments for over 500 MW of compute capacity capitalized independently by new investors.
insights.speaker: Vipul Ved Prakash
insights.so_what: The off-balance-sheet 500MW compute financing structure (energy/hardware investors like Schneider, Pegatron, Aramco) is a template for how neocloud-adjacent AI firms fund capex without diluting equity.
insights.quote: we have secured commitments for over 500 MW of compute capacity to be capitalized independently by our new investors
insights.locator: Today, I am excited to announce

insights.claim: Together AI's named customers include Cognition, Decagon, Eleven Labs, Cursor, and Suno, positioning it as infrastructure for the fastest-growing AI application companies.
insights.speaker: Vipul Ved Prakash
insights.so_what: Customer roster signals which AI-native startups run on open-model infra; a proxy for the open-inference market's momentum.

insights.claim: The cost structure of closed frontier LLMs that looks manageable in a prototype becomes unsustainable in production as inference bills compound faster than budgets, forcing companies to ration intelligence just as demand accelerates.
insights.speaker: Vipul Ved Prakash
insights.so_what: Frames closed-model inference cost as the structural wedge driving open-model adoption; the core bull case for open-weights infra providers.
insights.quote: As usage grows, inference bills compound faster than budgets, forcing companies to ration intelligence just as demand for it accelerates.

insights.claim: Companies building with open models routinely achieve 6x to 20x lower costs while maintaining equal or better performance, with Decagon cited as cutting inference costs sixfold after moving to Together AI.
insights.speaker: Vipul Ved Prakash
insights.so_what: A concrete cost-delta claim; if durable, it pressures margins of closed-model API vendors as production workloads migrate.
insights.quote: companies building with open models routinely achieve 6x to 20x lower costs while maintaining equal or better performance

insights.claim: Open-weights models DeepSeek, Nemotron, MiniMax, Kimi, and GLM have closed the quality gap with proprietary frontier models while allowing customization and fine-tuning.
insights.speaker: Vipul Ved Prakash
insights.so_what: Names the specific open models Together bets on; note the roster is heavily Chinese-origin (DeepSeek, MiniMax, Kimi, GLM), a geopolitical/sourcing signal.

insights.claim: Together argues production AI economics are set by the full stack (models, kernels, compilers, inference systems, training infra, hardware utilization), not models alone, positioning itself as a research-driven optimizer rather than a reseller.
insights.speaker: Vipul Ved Prakash
insights.so_what: The full-stack framing is the moat argument versus commodity GPU rental; evaluate rivals on kernel/compiler depth, not just capacity.
insights.quote: They are determined by the full stack: models, kernels, compilers, inference systems, training infrastructure, hardware utilization, and the software that brings all of it together.
insights.locator: But the economics of production AI

insights.claim: Together shipped FlashAttention-4 for NVIDIA Blackwell, Together Megakernel, together.compile, and expanded post-training APIs for tool calling, reasoning, and vision-language models, claiming to be one of the world's largest producers of AI tokens.
insights.speaker: Vipul Ved Prakash
insights.so_what: FlashAttention-4 on Blackwell shows Together's optimization work is tied to Nvidia's newest silicon; token-volume claim is a scale proxy but unquantified.
